steps.title: Find the rate of change per year
steps.content: The population increases from 55 thousand in 1995 to 217 thousand in 2015. That's a span of $2015 - 1995 = 20$ years with a change of $217 - 55 = 162$ thousand. The rate of change is $$\text{slope} = \frac{162}{20} = 8.1 \text{ thousand per year}$$

steps.title: Extend the line to 2019
steps.content: From 2015 to 2019 is $2019 - 2015 = 4$ years. At 8.1 thousand per year, the additional population is $$4 \times 8.1 = 32.4 \text{ thousand}$$ So the 2019 population is $$x = 217 + 32.4 = 249.4$$ To the nearest whole number, $x = 249$.
